title: How much screen time did Rei get compared to Asuka and Shinji
display_title: How much screen time did Rei get compared to Asuka and Shinji
wikitext:
[]: How much screen time did Rei get compared to Asuka and Shinji?             Quote Post by mrptkv99 » Thu Dec 24, 2020 9:10 am I feel like she wasn't at all active in the anime and didn't have many appearances compared to the other main characters which confused me because she is so universally liked

[]: Re: How much screen time did Rei get compared to Asuka and Shinji?             Quote Post by JoelcrNeto » Thu Dec 24, 2020 9:41 am Rei loved for her personality, as Asuka is explosive and unbearable. Rei doesn't really have a lot of screen time, but she was a very important character as she creates connections with Shinji in relation to Yui and Gendo. Its visibility is higher in EoE.

[]: Re: How much screen time did Rei get compared to Asuka and Shinji?             Quote Post by dzzthink » Thu Dec 24, 2020 11:13 am She doesn't need to have a lengthy appearance on screen to appreciate her importance in the series. She plays a rather nuanced role as the connection to the other-worldly nature of the Evas, and a major focus of the series is trying to understand her backstory. Quantitatively, the episodes where she is featured as a central character in the story is fewer than the other two: Shinji: 1, 2, 3, 4, 16, 18, 19, 20, 24, 26 Asuka: 8, 9, 10, 22 Rei: 5, 6, 23

[]: Re: How much screen time did Rei get compared to Asuka and Shinji?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Dec 24, 2020 12:56 pm So, both get a bit of focus around the time they're introduced and then when it comes time for them to go through the meatgrinder, that actually seems to be equal. Also there's not just quantity but quality - Extreme example: Kaworu had 1 episode and everyone loves him. Conversely, the bridge bunnies get lots of lines but most of those are technobabble unrelated to themselves. Raw time is not the only factor here. Asuka is a lot more comedic or 'filler' scenes, for example, but as far as the big overarching plot goes she is just a foot soldier, though her conflict with Shinji has thematic importance as a standing for his struggles with the difficulties of human interaction in the first place. Meanwhile, Rei has a lot of scenes that tell you a whole lot even if they are relatively short (episode 11 comes to mind... or really, anything from 14 to 19) Also, they're very different characters that demand different presentations which are going to appeal to different sorts of people.

[]: Re: How much screen time did Rei get compared to Asuka and Shinji?             Quote Post by mrptkv99 » Thu Dec 24, 2020 1:08 pm Kendrix wrote: So, both get a bit of focus around the time they're introduced and then when it comes time for them to go through the meatgrinder, that actually seems to be equal. Also there's not just quantity but quality - Extreme example: Kaworu had 1 episode and everyone loves him. Conversely, the bridge bunnies get lots of lines but most of those are technobabble unrelated to themselves. Raw time is not the only factor here. Asuka is a lot more comedic or 'filler' scenes, for example, but as far as the big overarching plot goes she is just a foot soldier, though her conflict with Shinji has thematic importance as a standing for his struggles with the difficulties of human interaction in the first place. Meanwhile, Rei has a lot of scenes that tell you a whole lot even if they are relatively short (episode 11 comes to mind... or really, anything from 14 to 19) Also, they're very different characters that demand different presentations which are going to appeal to different sorts of people.

[]: Re: How much screen time did Rei get compared to Asuka and Shinji?             Quote Post by Jäeger » Fri Jan 01, 2021 12:16 pm mrptkv99 wrote: I feel like she wasn't at all active in the anime and didn't have many appearances compared to the other main characters which confused me because she is so universally liked

[]: Re: How much screen time did Rei get compared to Asuka and Shinji?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Jan 01, 2021 12:54 pm That is soooo taken out of context. At most he needed a few episodes to figure out how to continue her arc but it worked out just fine 'cause it just made her look mysterious (and it was natural enough to focus on the new characters for a bit when they've just been introduced) For proper context, he also said he forgot about Shinji at some point during making the Rebuilds despite the fact that they're largely structured around him more than the original series is (or rather because of that, for the some reason I sometimes forget if I'm wearing glasses) Interestingness is like, to some extent in the eye of the beholder. And probably the reason most ppl liked her. Consider this: Maybe introverted, intellectual types like her because... she is one? I don't see how she's in any way portrayed like some unrealistic pleasant girlfriend material other than that she is... marginally nice to the protagonist sometimes, and vaguely gives a crap wether he lives or dies? And not "drops everything to be with him" nice, just "had a few supportive conversations with him here, after he put in major effort, and then it all ends badly" If you're looking for "unrealistic pleasant girlfriend material" or "otaku fantasy" material in fiction, I would look at... every bland satellite love interest or cutesy blob character ever... before even considering Rei, canonically a gloomy, unapproachable loner with no social skills, no special attractiveness and, like, two friends if you count Touji, and is usually the one to give the "brutal honesty" answer. All fiction is eventually fantasy even if it's "lecturing people about politics" or "experiencing extreme emotions in a safe setting"; That's not the clever gotcha you think it is and certainly not what the show is saying (spoiler: It's "know thyself" not "man up") You know how Cat people say "well both are nice but I prefer cats" but dog people will try to convince you that cats are evil? There's a segment of Asuka fans that is exactly like dog people, though this seems to be an anglosphere exclusive phenomenon insofar as I can tell

[]: Re: How much screen time did Rei get compared to Asuka and Shinji?             Quote Post by Jäeger » Fri Jan 01, 2021 1:20 pm Kendrix wrote: That is soooo taken out of context. At most he needed a few episodes to figure out how to continue her arc but it worked out just fine 'cause it just made her look mysterious (and it was natural enough to focus on the new characters for a bit when they've just been introduced) For proper context, he also said he forgot about Shinji at some point during making the Rebuilds despite the fact that they're largely structured around him more than the original series is (or rather because of that, for the some reason I sometimes forget if I'm wearing glasses) Interestingness is like, to some extent in the eye of the beholder. And probably the reason most ppl liked her. You know how Cat people say "well both are nice but I prefer cats" but dog people will try to convince you that cats are evil? There's a segment of Asuka fans that is exactly like dog people, though this seems to be an anglosphere exclusive phenomenon insofar as I can tell

[]: Re: How much screen time did Rei get compared to Asuka and Shinji?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Jan 01, 2021 1:37 pm Jäeger wrote: She is not as "complex" as Shinji Asuka o Misato

[]: Re: How much screen time did Rei get compared to Asuka and Shinji?             Quote Post by Zusuchan » Fri Jan 01, 2021 1:41 pm She is not as "complex" as Shinji, Asuka o Misato (she has issues from being a clone, but not a real drama behind her) and her role benefits from her lack of interactions.

[]: Re: How much screen time did Rei get compared to Asuka and Shinji?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Jan 01, 2021 1:45 pm The only type of scenes that Rei really gets less of is comedic scenes, but that's more a flavor thing; Gendo doesn't get them either. (whereas other characters like Misato, Asuka, Touji, Kensuke etc. practically need funny, banal scenesto create that 'found family like' feeling there - but the characterization content in a long, silly scene vs. a short, serious one is usually about the same) Ah, and because I forget, the "forgetting about Shinji" thing is here in the rebuild 2 CR -> https://www.gwern.net/docs/eva/2010-crc# where the whole Rei writing thing is also explained - He considers Rei and Shinji "uninteresting" in the sense that they're basically him . You're not that "interested" in your reflection because you already know what you look like. Maybe imagining the life of a hot, hypercompetent chick like Misato and Asuka is more interesting lol We'd like to be cool-ass Misatos and Asukas, but in reality most of us are probably closer to being awkward little Shinjis and Reis drudgering away at our desks

[]: Re: How much screen time did Rei get compared to Asuka and Shinji?             Quote Post by mrptkv99 » Sat Jan 02, 2021 1:35 pm Jäeger wrote: [ She is a totally secondary character for almost the whole series. And not for 3 or 4 episodes.....until ep 23. I'm not despising the character talking this way, because IHMO is the only way it can work in the story. She is not as "complex" as Shinji, Asuka o Misato (she has issues from being a clone, but not a real drama behind her) and her role benefits from her lack of interactions . ...

[]: Re: How much screen time did Rei get compared to Asuka and Shinji?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Jan 02, 2021 4:39 pm I'm not sure if "despise" is per se the right word, it's more that this group tends to have this very narrow interpretation of her. She's only a plot device because she's not relatable, and if you find her relatable, you must be just projecting because she is only a plot device, the wheels on the bus circular reasoning goes round & round I don't even see the "She's irrelevant until EoE" thing. I mean there's a lot of her physical likeness in there and some moments where we get insight into what Shinji thinks of her, but she only really has 2 scenes of import: a) Betraying Gendo b) The dialogue during 3rd impact IDK how that counts in a way that all the earlier moments throughout the series don't, such as - something happens, all three pilots respond somehow, the contrast tells us something about each - Prolonged one on one conversation (eg. Shinji cleans her apartment in ep 17, or she gets into that argument with Asuka in ep 16) ... I wouldn't say every scene where Misato & Ritsuko discuss the latest angel count as such, only the ones where we get new info or emphasized reactions (such as ep 12's "revenge" conversation) - memorable action moments (eg. *charges Zeruel with N2 mine*, *leads the other two to the cages during the blackout*) I guess ultimately we'd get into some discussion into what you count as 'development' or 'screentime' There are many ways of doing character exposition and not every tool is appropriate for every type of character.

[]: Re: How much screen time did Rei get compared to Asuka and Shinji?             Quote Post by Berserker » Sun Jan 03, 2021 1:03 am mrptkv99 wrote: She gets less scenes because she's not human and doesn't have human connections and her background couldn't be explored as much as the other characters with "normal" lives and experiences.

[]: Re: How much screen time did Rei get compared to Asuka and Shinji?             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Wed Jan 06, 2021 3:24 pm Rei Ayanami, huh? Not really my favorite character, but I can understand her appeal, I guess. The first time I watched Evangelion, I remember not thinking too much about her. Compared to characters like Asuka, Shinji, Misato and even Gendo, she was just not interesting to me. But the fact that she had less screen time was not what made me think like that. Ayanami is really important for the part of Evangelion that I care least about, the lore. If you put her against Misato or Asuka, she definitely holds more weight in this regard. But it's in character-to-character moments that she tends not to impress me. She simply has way less "mundane" scenes to work with. Rei also has a smaller number of characters with whom she interacts. As a result, she has fewer moments in which I feel personally invested in her character. None of her scenes were ever able to grab my attention like the kiss between Shinji and Asuka did. Now is that because she had less screen time to work with? No. Is this even a problem? Not at all! That's just a reflection of how her character was written to be and I respect that. In fact, considering all of this, she still had a bunch of cool moments throughout the series (specially in episodes 5, 6 and 23). It's just that they were less common when compared to other characters. By the way, I know that's a very subjective read of the character. I'm not saying that everyone has to see her in the same way as I do. This is just what I feel while watching her in action.
summary:
[]: How much screen time did Rei get compared to Asuka and Shinji?             Quote Post by mrptkv99 » Thu Dec 24, 2020 9:10 am I feel like she wasn't at all active in the anime and didn't have many appearances compared to the other main characters which confused me because she is so universally liked

[]: Re: How much screen time did Rei get compared to Asuka and Shinji?             Quote

Article: How much screen time did Rei get compared to Asuka and Shinji
     Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Wed Jan 06, 2021 3:24 pm Rei Ayanami, huh? Not really my favorite character, but I can understand her appeal, I guess. The first time I watched Evangelion, I remember not thinking too much about her. Compared to characters like Asuka, Shinji, Misato and even Gendo, she was just not interesting to me. But the fact that she had less screen time was not what made me think like that. Ayanami is really important for the part of Evangelion that I care least about, the lore. If you put her against Misato or Asuka, she definitely holds more weight in this regard. But it's in character-to-character moments that she tends not to impress me. She simply has way less "mundane" scenes to work with. Rei also has a smaller number of characters with whom she interacts. As a result, she has fewer moments in which I feel personally invested in her character. None of her scenes were ever able to grab my attention like the kiss between Shinji and Asuka did. Now is that because she had less screen time to work with? No. Is this even a problem? Not at all! That's just a reflection of how her character was written to be and I respect that. In fact, considering all of this, she still had a bunch of cool moments throughout the series (specially in episodes 5, 6 and 23). It's just that they were less common when compared to other characters. By the way, I know that's a very subjective read of the character. I'm not saying that everyone has to see her in the same way as I do. This is just what I feel while watching her in action.
